items: A public market tracker reported that utility-scale battery storage demand continued to expand in the Southwest during May 2026, with project pipeline growing 12% quarter-over-quarter.
A policy update indicated that new interconnection queue reforms may shorten approval timelines for selected renewable projects by up to 6 months, potentially accelerating deployment schedules.
A competitor announced a 2 GW manufacturing capacity expansion plan, with commercial production expected in 2027. The expansion represents a $400 million capital investment.
Industry analysts noted that supply chain constraints for lithium carbonate have eased compared to Q1 2026, with spot prices declining 8% month-over-month.
A regional utility signed a 200 MWh storage purchase agreement with a preferred vendor, marking the largest single procurement in the Southwest this quarter.
The Department of Energy released updated guidance on domestic content requirements for battery storage systems eligible for the Investment Tax Credit, effective Q3 2026.
Solar-plus-storage project completions in Texas reached a record 1.8 GW in Q1 2026, driven by favorable wholesale market conditions and declining component costs.
A major grid operator announced plans to add 15 GW of new transmission capacity across the Western Interconnection by 2029, with priority given to renewable energy zones.
FERC issued a final rule requiring regional transmission organizations to reform their interconnection queue processes, mandating withdrawal penalties for stalled projects.
A leading battery manufacturer reported a 22% year-over-year increase in energy storage system shipments, citing strong demand from commercial and industrial customers.
The California Public Utilities Commission approved a 4.5 GWh procurement mandate for utility-scale storage by 2028, expanding the state's existing storage targets.
European battery cell imports to the US increased 18% in Q1 2026 as domestic manufacturers continued to ramp up production capacity under new IRA incentives.
A renewable energy developer secured $1.2 billion in project financing for a portfolio of 800 MW solar and 400 MWh storage projects across three states.
Wind energy curtailment rates in the Southwest dropped to 3.2% in April 2026, down from 5.1% in the same period last year, reflecting improved grid integration.
A consortium of utilities announced a joint procurement for 2 GWh of long-duration energy storage, targeting iron-air and flow battery technologies for 2027-2028 delivery.
The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission approved rate incentives for transmission projects that integrate renewable energy zones, expected to accelerate grid buildout in underserved regions.
